title: Delicious Japanese Rice Takikomi-Gohan
description: A floral and shattering one-pot Japanese dish made with low-glycemic-friendly ingredients, perfect for any meal of the day. This recipe features a gherkin relish and grilled flavors that will leave you wanting more.
instructions: Heat sesame oil in a large clay pot or Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add grated ginger and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add miso paste and cook for an additional minute, stirring constantly.
Add soy sauce, salt, and water to the pot, and stir to combine.
Add Japanese short-grain rice to the pot, and stir to coat the rice with the sauce.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and cover the pot with a tight-fitting lid.
Simmer for 15 minutes, or until the rice is cooked and the liquid has been absorbed.
Add chopped gherkin, sliced grilled shiitake mushrooms, and grated daikon to the pot, and stir to combine.
Cook for an additional 2 minutes, until the ingredients are heated through.
Stir in chopped parsley, and serve hot.
Transfer the takikomi-gohan to a serving platter, and garnish with additional parsley and grilled shiitake mushrooms, if desired.
